heading: Recognition of gain on certain transfers to certain foreign trusts and estates
label: 684 Recognition of gain on certain transfers to certain foreign trusts and estates
notes: CodificationAnother amended sections 367, 721, and 1035 of this title.section 1131(b) of Pub. L. 105–34

Amendments2010— amended catchline, introductory provisions of subsec. (a), and subsec. (b) to read as if amendment by –(C), had never been enacted. See 2001 Amendment note below. Prior to amendment, subsec. (b) read as follows: “.—Pub. L. 111–312Pub. L. 107–16, § 542(e)(1)(A)Exceptions“(1) .—Subsection (a) shall not apply to a transfer to a trust by a United States person to the extent that any United States person is treated as the owner of such trust under section 671.Transfers to certain trusts“(2) .—Subsection (a) shall not apply to a lifetime transfer to a nonresident alien.”Lifetime transfers to nonresident aliens2001—–(C), amended section by inserting “and nonresident aliens” after “estates” in section catchline and “or to a nonresident alien” after “or trust” in introductory provisions of subsec. (a) and amending subsec. (b) generally. Prior to amendment, text of subsec. (b) read as follows: “Subsection (a) shall not apply to a transfer to a trust by a United States person to the extent that any person is treated as the owner of such trust under section 671.”Pub. L. 107–16, § 542(e)(1)(A)

children.text: the fair market value of the property so transferred, over
the adjusted basis (for purposes of determining gain) of such property in the hands of the transferor.

children.heading: Exception
children.label: b Exception
children.text: Subsection (a) shall not apply to a transfer to a trust by a United States person to the extent that any person is treated as the owner of such trust under section 671.

children.heading: Treatment of trusts which become foreign trusts
children.label: c Treatment of trusts which become foreign trusts
children.text: If a trust which is not a foreign trust becomes a foreign trust, such trust shall be treated for purposes of this section as having transferred, immediately before becoming a foreign trust, all of its assets to a foreign trust.
